results.title: lululemon Applied AI/ML Scientist job with lululemon | 313183
highlights:
# lululemon Applied AI/ML Scientist job with lululemon | 313183
[...]
# lululemon Applied AI/ML Scientist
[...]
Description & Requirements Who we are lululemon is an innovative performance apparel company for yoga, running, training, and other athletic pursuits. Setting the bar in technical fabrics and functional design, we create transformational products and experiences that support people in moving, growing, connecting, and being well. We owe our success to our innovative product, emphasis on stores, commitment to our people, and the incredible connections we make in every community we're in. As a company, we focus on creating positive change to build a healthier, thriving future. That includes creating an equitable, inclusive and growth-focused environment for our people. About this team The Enterprise Data & AI team is a strategic and operational driver of growth for lululemon, owning and building the data and AI platforms and products that enable the enterprise to operate with intelligence at scale. The team leads the design and delivery of a trusted unified data foundation, advanced analytics capabilities, and AI solutions across lululemon's vertically integrated retail ecosystem, embedding strong data governance and responsible AI practices from the very beginning. By applying AI to critical business challenges and creating new, transformative AI solutions, the team helps reshape how lululemon operates. Through deep partnership with product, technology, and business teams, Enterprise Data & AI accelerates product innovation, unlocks measurable value, elevates guest and educator experiences, and drives enterprise efficiency. Core responsibilities As an Applied AI/ML Scientist, you design, develop, test, and optimize AI/ML solutions that address defined business problems, with an emphasis on applied modeling, experimentation, and translating advanced AI research into scalable, production-ready solutions. You work independently to design and adapt AI/ML approaches to build prototypes, and improve model performance, reliability, and efficiency. You use domain knowledge and collaborate with stakeholders and product teams to map the business problem to the right model and AI/ML solution approach. You own the AI solution implementation and partner with engineering teams to integrate solutions into the broader engineering system. You contribute to solution enhancements, validate outcomes, and continuously improve models based on real world performance. Examples of projects could be
[...]
- Designing and deploying machine learning and predictive/generative AI models for demand forecasting, optimization, personalization and search, intelligent automation, and multimodal content creation and enrichment
[...]
- Applying NLP, computer vision, multimodal, or time series modeling techniques to enterprise-scale datasets that are a mix of structured and unstructured data
[...]
- Developing experimentation frameworks and evaluation approaches to measure business impact of AI solutions in production
[...]
- Collaborating with product and engineering teams to embed AI models into end to end business workflows
[...]
AI/ML engineers
[...]
- Bachelor's degree in computer science, machine learning, mathematics, or related field, Masters preferred
[...]
- 4-8 years of experience building and optimizing AI/ML solutions, or equivalent research experience, including educational experience (e.g., Master's or PhD degree)
[...]
- Demonstrated experience in evaluating and applying state-of-the-art AI/ML models and methods to develop practical, high-performing solutions to specific use-cases.
[...]
- Experience designing AI/ML approaches, conducting experiments to evaluate performance, iterating on solution design to improve performance, and translating AI-ML driven insights into business goals
[...]
- Experience with ML implementation using commonly used tools such as Python, pyTorch, cloud ML platforms and libraries, open-source toolkits.

results.title: Applied Data Scientist | Hirequorum
highlights:
Applied Data Scientist | Hirequorum
[...]
# Applied Data Scientist
[...]
Are you passionate about using data science, artificial intelligence, and advanced analytics to propel decisions? If you are interested in driving meaningful change for a growth focused consumer products brand, this is an opportunity to join the market-leading Delta Faucet Company team as we use analytics to turbocharge strategic insights!
[...]
Reporting to the Manager of Data Science and Analytics, this role will work with analytics and domain specialists across the business to deliver high-impact insights and data science solutions. You will develop advanced analytical models, experimentation tests, applied AI techniques, and partner with business stakeholders to translate complex data into clear, actionable recommendations that support the company’s growth initiatives.
[...]
As an Applied Data Scientist within Delta Faucet Company’s Analytics Center of Excellence (ACoE), you will act as an owner of high impact analysis and data science initiatives that support complex, ambiguous business decisions. This role focuses on advanced analytics, experimentation, and applied AI.
[...]
- Independently lead end-to-end analytics and data science projects, from problem framing through delivery of actionable insights.
- Apply statistical methods, machine learning, and analytical modeling to solve business problems related to growth, customer behavior, market dynamics, and operational efficiency.
- Develop and maintain analytical data models and curated datasets, partnering with data engineering and BI teams.
- Build AI and machine learning solutions that are aligned with business needs.
- Collaborate closely with business stakeholders to translate analytical findings into clear recommendations.
- Present data-driven recommendations to leaders across the organization, including tactical and C-suite.
[...]
- 4+ years of experience in data science, advanced analytics, or applied machine learning roles.
- 4+ years of hands-on experience applying statistical methods and analytical modeling to real business problems.
- 4+ years of experience using Python for data analysis, modeling, and experimentation.
- 4+ years of experience writing and refining complex SQL queries against large, structured datasets.
- 3+ years of experience partnering directly with business stakeholders to translate analytical findings into actionable recommendations.
- 2+ years of experience working in a modern analytics platform (e.g., Databricks, Spark/PySpark, or similar).
- 1+ years of hands-on‑ experience applying generative AI technologies (e.g., LLM APIs, task‑specific GenAI workflows) in an enterprise analytics environment.
[...]
- Bachelor’s degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Data Science, Economics, or a related quantitative field.
[...]
- 2+ years of experience with analytics engineering and data modeling, including building reusable, well-documented analytical datasets; familiarity with medallion architecture (bronze/silver/gold) preferred.
- 1+ years of experience with model monitoring, experiment tracking, and model evaluation in production settings.
- 1+ years of experience with conjoint analysis or other stated-preference research methods.

results.title: Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore
highlights:
Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore
[...]
# Data Scientist
[...]
Optum is seeking a Data Science professional who is passionate about applying machine learning, Generative AI, and Large Language Models (LLMs) to solve complex healthcare problems on a scale. Imagine working with massive volumes of healthcare data spanning clinical, claims, behavioral, and digital interactions – powering intelligent personalization, discovery, and decision support for millions of members, providers, and care teams.
[...]
At Optum, the Data Science mission is to transform healthcare data into robust, scalable AI solutions, including LLM based and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) systems that combine enterprise knowledge with advanced language models to deliver accurate, contextual, and trustworthy insights. These solutions help uncover deep usage patterns, member journeys, and care signals that inform and continuously evolve Optum’s digital products, clinical platforms, and healthcare services.
[...]
Your work will directly influence how healthcare consumers engage with digital experiences, how clinicians access timely insights, and how business and care strategies are shaped through data. By joining Optum’s data science team, you will be part of an exciting journey to build advanced ML and GenAI driven products, grounded in responsible AI principles, that operate at enterprise scale and create meaningful impact across the healthcare ecosystem.
[...]
- Design, develop, and deploy advanced analytics, machine learning, and Generative AI solutions to address complex healthcare and business problems using largescale structured and unstructured data
[...]
- Applies advanced techniques including statistics
[...]
machine learning,
[...]
- Build and optimize LLM based applications, including Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) frameworks, to enable accurate, context aware, and explainable insights from enterprise healthcare data sources such as clinical notes, claims, provider data, and policy documents
[...]
- Develop, evaluate, and finetune machine learning and GenAI models, including prompt engineering, retrieval strategies, and model evaluation techniques to improve accuracy, relevance, and performance
[...]
- Translate complex analytical and GenAI outputs into clear, actionable insights for business stakeholders, product teams, and leadership to support data driven decision making
[...]
- Support prototyping, validation, and deployment of GenAI and ML solutions, working with engineering teams to ensure solutions are scalable, secure, and maintainable. Continuously stay current with advancements in LLMs, GenAI frameworks, and healthcare AI trends, contributing innovative ideas and improvements to Optum’s capabilities
[...]
### Required Qualifications:
[...]
- 4+ years of experience in handling/active development in data science projects
[...]
- 4+ years of experience in Microsoft Azure Cloud, Databricks, Mlflow, Spark SQL & Python
[...]
- Experience using Machine Learning Algorithms: Linear & Logistic Regression, Time Series analysis, Support Vector Machine, K-Means clustering, K-Nearest Neighbors Decision Trees, Random Forest, Naive Bayes, PCA, SVD, Artificial Neural Networks, Association Rules, Genetic Algorithm, Optimization, Bagging and Boosting algorithms etc.
[...]
- Understanding of data, schema, data model, machine learning and how to bring efficiency in big data related life cycle
[...]
- Proven skills to create opportunities using machine learning or artificial intelligence
[...]
Optum, part of UnitedHealth Group, is a global health services and technology company revolutionizing healthcare delivery through data analytics, pharmacy benefits, and digital health solutions that connect millions to better care. Headquartered in Eden Prairie, Minnesota, Optum serves patients, providers, payers, and pharmacies worldwide with innovative platforms for care optimization, claims processing, and population health management.

results.title: Senior Data Scientist at Rue Gilt Groupe
highlights:
Senior Data Scientist at Rue Gilt Groupe
[...]
### Rue Gilt Groupe
[...]
# Senior Data Scientist
[...]
### Job Description
[...]
Senior Data Scientist (RGG Services, Inc., Boston, Massachusetts):
[...]
Will work with the Data Science team to build innovative data science that power our online retail business.
[...]
- Work on challenging data science projects including Generative AI, NLP, image recognition, recommendation systems, advanced statistical prediction models, and other AI technologies to drive revenue and enhance personalized shopping experiences of our 50 million members.
[...]
- Identify and lead strategic analytics and predictive modeling opportunities, leveraging AI and machine learning (ML) to optimize business processes and product offerings.
[...]
- Work with a modern and robust technology stack, including Spark, TensorFlow, PyTorch, Snowflake, Airflow, and AWS services, to enable cloud-based data processing and machine learning at scale for internal and external use cases.
[...]
- Analyze problems to identify new AI opportunities leveraging data-driven insights involving cutting edge technology and software.
[...]
- Brainstorm next-generation AI product ideas with other data scientists and engineers to build exciting projects used by millions of our customers daily.
[...]
- Responsible for evaluating data science and AI strategies and software solutions to determine technical feasibility, ensure scalability, and validate alignment with organizational objectives.
[...]
to design and implement cross-functional
[...]
- Responsible for identifying, evaluating, and integrating emerging technologies and tools to advance the AI platform, with a focus on expanding Generative AI applications and improving the performance, scalability, and applicability of AI/ML systems for personalization and recommendation.
[...]
- Responsible for leading research initiatives, translating complex machine learning and AI research papers into functional code, prototypes, and production-ready models to solve advanced business challenges and enhance AI/ML algorithmic capabilities.
[...]
Minimum Requirements: Master’s degree in in Data Science, Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or a closely related technical field and 2 years of experience in an occupation with advanced data analytics.
[...]
Must also have: (I) Must have industry or academic experience delivering software development, machine learning, and data science projects end-to-end; (2) Strong knowledge of system design, data structures, algorithms, and software engineering best practices; (3) Demonstrated fluency in Python and SQL and background producing clean, testable, and well-documented code; (4) Background: (a) using Spark and AWS ecosystem; (b) using Infrastructure as code tools including Cloudformation and Terraform; (c) working with structured and unstructured data and table design; and (d) working in an agile environment producing deliverables in a quick and iterative manner; (5) Demonstrated expertise in one or more of: recommendation systems, NLP/LLMs, computer vision, or predictive modeling for consumer-facing applications. (6) Background: (a) using modern ML frameworks including PyTorch and/or TensorFlow; and
[...]
(b) using GPU-accelerated training; (7) Track record leading research initiatives, including the ability to translate complex academic research papers into functional, production-ready code to solve real-world business problems; (8) Track record of excellent communication skills and the ability to work well in a close-knit team with a collaborative spirit.
[...]
Salary: $154,606-$164,606. Apply by resume only to avillalobos@ruegiltgroupe.com including NC10216 in subject line. Or apply online at https://careers.ruegiltgroupe.com/ (RGG Services, Inc., is a Rue Gilt Groupe Company). Search under “Technology” section to access this Senior Data Scientist posting. An EOE/AAE.

results.title: AI Data Scientist | Jobflarely
highlights:
AI Data Scientist | Jobflarely

Jobflarely.← All Jobs

# AI Data Scientist

Posted Jun 9, 2026

Burlington Stores, Inc. is a growing company committed to being an employer of choice. They are seeking a Junior AI Data Scientist to support various business areas by analyzing data and developing AI-driven solutions to enhance retail operations and customer experiences.

### Responsibilities

- Work with stakeholders throughout the organization including Merchandising, Allocations, Marketing, IT and Supply Chain Analytics to assist in developing and implementing AI-powered solutions
- Mine and analyze structured and unstructured data from the data warehouse
- Support prompt engineering and optimization for large language models (LLMs)
- Integrate AI services via APIs and cloud AI platforms
- Keep up to date with latest technology trends
- Work closely with senior data scientists to expand technical and functional knowledge as well as apply best practices
- Coordinate with different functional teams to identify AI automation opportunities
- Develop processes and tools to monitor and analyze model performance and data accuracy
- Translate complex analytical findings into clear business recommendations

Skills

- Bachelor's degree in AI, Data Science, Computer Science, or related field
- 0–2 years of experience in AI, data analytics, or related technical role
- Knowledge in Large Language Models (LLMs), Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), and AI model evaluation concepts
- Exposure to cloud AI platforms (Azure AI, AWS AI services, or GCP Vertex AI)
- 2+ years Data Science experience with extensive background in building ML models, data mining and statistical analysis
- Strong interpersonal and relationship building skills
- Strong written and verbal communication skills

### Benefits

- Competitive wage and benefit package
- Generous paid time off plan
- Company matched 401(k)
- Associate discount
- Medical, dental and vision coverage
- Life and disability insurance
- Paid holidays
- Training and development opportunities

Company Overview

- Burlington Stores, Inc., headquartered in New Jersey, is a nationally recognized off-price retailer. It was founded in 1972, and is headquartered in Burlington, New Jersey, USA, with a workforce of 10001+ employees. Its website is https://www.burlington.com/. Company H1B Sponsorship

- Burlington Stores, Inc. has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 2 in 2026, 4 in 2025, 2 in 2024, 7 in 2023, 7 in 2022, 4 in 2021, 4 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.

results.title: Lead AI Individual Contributor Data Scientist at USAA - United State | Visa Sponsorship Jobs
highlights:
# Lead AI Individual Contributor Data Scientist
[...]
USAA seeks a Lead AI Individual Contributor Data Scientist to drive strategic adoption and practical application of AI, particularly Generative AI, across USAA Federal Savings Bank. The role involves identifying and implementing AI solutions to boost productivity, automate processes, and create new revenue streams. The ideal candidate will have 6+ years of experience in predictive analytics or data analysis, with a strong background in machine learning, simulation, and optimization.
[...]
Develops scalable, automated solutions using machine learning, simulation, and optimization to deliver business insights and business value.
[...]
Machine learning Simulation Optimization Python R SQL HQL NoSQL Linear/logistic regression Discriminant analysis Support vector machines Decision trees Forest models K-means clustering Hierarchical/agglomerative clustering Neighbors algorithms DBSCAN
[...]
Why USAA?At USAA, our mission is to empower our members to achieve financial security through highly competitive products, exceptional service and trusted advice. We seek to be the #1 choice for the military community and their families.Embrace a fulfilling career at USAA, where our core values – honesty, integrity, loyalty and service – define how we treat each other and our members. Be part of what truly makes us special and impactful.We are proud to support active-duty military spouses. USAA roles may offer remote or hybrid flexibility for active-duty military spouses consistent with applicable policy and business needs.The OpportunityWe are seeking a Lead AI Individual Contributor Data Scientist to drive the strategic adoption and practical application of AI, particularly Generative AI, across USAA Federal Savings Bank (USAA FSB). You will identify and implement AI solutions – whether cutting-edge industry advancements, existing solutions from other USAA lines of business, or tools that connect and integrate various platforms and IT systems – to boost productivity, automate processes, reduce costs, and create new revenue streams within the financial services context. This includes actively scouting for and evaluating external AI tools and technologies through various channels, such as professional networks and ties to academia, AI events, conferences, academic research, and a keen awareness of technological advancements, to ensure we are leveraging the most innovative solutions available. These efforts will encompass exploring AI solutions that serve as intelligent work assistants, streamlining and optimizing routine tasks for our employees, ultimately enhancing the experience and value delivered to our USAA members, while also considering the visionary potential and ethical implications of these advancements.This role is remote eligible in the continental U.S. with occasional business travel. However, individuals residing within a 60-mile radius of a USAA office will be expected to work on-site four days per week.Relocation assistance is available for this position.What You'll Do
[...]
- 4 years of experience in training and validating statistical, physical, machine learning, and other advanced analytics models.
[...]
- 4 years of experience in one or more dynamic scripted language (such as Python, R, etc.) for performing statistical analyses and/or building and scoring AI/ML models.
[...]
, etc.
[...]
- Experience in AI/ML/Data Science, focused on product development, strategy, or modeling.
[...]
- Experience with developing and implementing AI solutions across diverse platforms and within complex data infrastructures.
[...]
python nosql ai xml simulation aws ai tools sql data analysis machine learning

results.title: Sr. Data Scientist, Gen AI Automation | Hirevector
highlights:
Sr. Data Scientist, Gen AI Automation | Hirevector
[...]
# Sr. Data Scientist, Gen AI Automation
[...]
At Pinterest, AI isn't just a feature, it's a powerful partner that augments our creativity and amplifies our impact, and we’re looking for candidates who are excited to be a part of that. To get a complete picture of your experience and abilities, we’ll explore your foundational skills and how you collaborate with AI.
[...]
Are you passionate about applying GenAI to improve efficiency and accelerate business execution—grounded in strong data foundations? Join Pinterest’s IT Enterprise Systems team to build analytics engineering and GenAI capabilities that power our go-to-market and corporate operations. You’ll create trusted data models and pipelines across core enterprise platforms and layer in LLM-enabled workflows with clear evaluation, observability, and guardrails.
[...]
- Build production-grade GenAI-enabled analytics solutions that integrate enterprise data sources (e.g., Salesforce, Gong, Oracle, IBM Planning Analytics, and other SaaS platforms).
[...]
- Establish end-to-end LLM pipelines (retrieval/orchestration) with evaluation frameworks, observability, and validation guardrails to ensure reliability and safety.
[...]
- Lead technical scoping for GenAI use cases, assessing feasibility, accuracy expectations, risk, and ROI—then translating into clear technical plans.
[...]
- Design and own analytics-ready data models, including dimensional modeling (star schemas, fact/dimension tables, SCD Type 2) that support reporting, forecasting, and downstream applications.
[...]
- Develop and maintain robust data pipelines and orchestration (Airflow and/or dbt or similar), including data quality checks, SLAs, monitoring, and failure recovery.
[...]
- Write and optimize complex SQL for transformation and analysis across enterprise datasets (joins, window functions, CTEs, performance tuning).
[...]
- Partner closely with business stakeholders, turning ambiguous questions into well-scoped requirements, success metrics, and deliverables; communicate tradeoffs and constraints clearly.
[...]
- Use AI tools to accelerate development responsibly (e.g., drafting SQL/Python, iterating on prompts, summarizing findings), while applying rigorous testing and verification.
[...]
- Define best practices for GenAI in analytics engineering, including prompt patterns for analytics tasks, model selection, and cost/latency optimization.
[...]
- What we're looking for:
[...]
- 8+ years in analytics engineering, data engineering, data science, or related roles (or equivalent experience).
- Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Data Science, Mathematics, or a related field (or equivalent experience).
[...]
- Strong understanding of LLM fundamentals and practical tradeoffs, including:
[...]
- Capabilities, limitations and common failure modes.
[...]
- Tokenization, context windows, latency/cost considerations.
[...]
- RAG vs. fine-tuning vs. prompting/tools for use cases.

# Senior Director, Data Science

Posted May 30, 2026

Full-time

Introduction: About Us: SymphonyAI is a leading enterprise AI solutions provider empowering retailers and manufacturers to optimize business operations through customer insights. Our innovative technology and strategic collaboration position us as a preferred partner for leading retailers and manufacturers globally. 

Job Description: About the role: Strategic Leadership: Lead and develop the Data Science and ML Engineering teams to deliver actionable business insights and production-grade ML solutions. Collaborate with senior leadership to define the analytics and AI roadmap aligned to retail business priorities (pricing, promotions, assortment, personalized marketing, and customer experience). Team Management and Mentoring: Build, mentor, and retain a high-performing team of data scientists and machine learning engineers. Foster a culture of innovation, continuous learning, and excellence. Solution Design and Delivery: Oversee end-to-end delivery of advanced analytics and machine learning projects, from ideation to deployment and ongoing optimization. Architect and implement scalable models for price optimization, promotion effectiveness, assortment planning, demand forecasting, and personalized marketing. Hands-on Technical Oversight: Guide the team in leveraging modern data science tools and frameworks (Python, R, Spark). Champion the adoption and integration of GenAI (Generative AI) for real-world retail applications (e.g., automated content creation, chatbot solutions, recommendation systems). Deployment & Integration: Drive best practices for robust model deployment and MLOps, ensuring seamless integration of models into retail software platforms or products. Work closely with product and engineering teams to operationalize data science solutions at scale. About you: 10+ years of industry experience in data science and machine learning; min 3+ years in a leadership role managing data science and/or ML engineering teams. Demonstrated expertise in retail analytics (price optimization, promotion analytics, assortment planning, personalization, customer segmentation, etc.). Advanced degree (PhD/Masters) in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Data Science, or related field. Extensive hands-on experience in Python (scikit-learn, pandas, TensorFlow/PyTorch), R, and Spark. Proven experience with designing, building, and deploying production-grade ML models and integrating with existing business software/systems. Strong understanding of MLOps, Cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure) and containerization (Docker, Kubernetes) is highly desirable. Experience with GenAI frameworks, applications, and responsible AI principles. 

About Us: What We Offer: Competitive salary and benefits package. Opportunities to work on innovative, cutting-edge technology projects. Supportive, collaborative, and technically enriching work environment. Professional growth and upskilling opportunities.

About The CompanyUSAA is a leading financial services organization dedicated to serving the unique needs of military members and their families. Our mission is to empower our members to achieve financial security through highly competitive products, exceptional service, and trusted advice. We strive to be the preferred choice within the military community by upholding our core values of honesty, integrity, loyalty, and service. USAA is committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive environment where our team members can grow and thrive. We also take pride in supporting active-duty military spouses, offering flexible work arrangements such as remote or hybrid options, in line with company policies and business needs. Our organization values innovation and continuous improvement, making us a dynamic and rewarding place to build your career.About The RoleWe are seeking a highly skilled Lead AI Individual Contributor Data Scientist to spearhead the strategic adoption and practical application of artificial intelligence, with a particular focus on Generative AI, within USAA Federal Savings Bank (USAA FSB). This pivotal role involves identifying innovative AI solutions, whether emerging industry technologies, existing tools from other USAA divisions, or integrated platform solutions, to enhance productivity, automate processes, reduce operational costs, and unlock new revenue streams in the financial services sector. The successful candidate will actively scout and evaluate external AI tools and technologies through various channels, including professional networks, academic collaborations, industry conferences, and research publications, ensuring USAA remains at the forefront of technological advancements. The role emphasizes the development of intelligent work assistants that streamline routine tasks, thereby improving the member experience and operational efficiency while considering ethical implications and future potential of AI innovations.This position offers remote work flexibility within the continental U.S., with occasional travel requirements. Candidates residing within a 60-mile radius of a USAA office are expected to work on-site four days a week. Relocation assistance is available for qualified applicants seeking to join our team.QualificationsThe ideal candidate will possess a strong educational background in a quantitative discipline such as mathematics, computer science, statistics, economics, finance, actuarial sciences, or engineering. A minimum of eight years of experience in predictive analytics or data analysis is required, with advanced degrees (Master’s or PhD) in relevant fields and at least six years of related experience preferred. Candidates should have extensive experience in training, validating, and deploying advanced analytics models, including machine learning, physical models, and statistical techniques.

 Job Description As the Senior AI Scientist, you will work alongside industry experts developing and applying cutting edge ML & AI algorithms to optimize Best Buy customer experience. The systems you build will power customer experiences responsible for billions of dollars in revenues from home page, search, recommendations to checkout and post-purchase. You will work in a dynamic team tackling a variety of use cases including understanding search intent, intelligently optimizing the page layout, deciding what products to be shown in carousels, trading off between giving customers what they want vs allowing for serendipity, etc. You will work alongside a fast-paced team enabling ideas to often go into production in a matter of weeks. This role is remote eligible, which means you would work virtually from home or another non-Best Buy location. What you’ll do Design and implement large-scale Search, RecSys, Personalization and Adtech systems that power customer experiences across Best Buy surfaces. Derive complex features from large scale clickstream logs for model training data Develop & finetune retrieval/ranking models using supervised learning, deep learning, LLMs, and generative AI (e.g., Two Tower models, Sequence models, GNNs, etc.) Design custom evaluation metrics for measuring quality of ML and GenAI models. Collaborate with AI engineers, platform and product teams to deploy low latency models. Basic qualifications Bachelor's degree in a highly quantitative field (Computer Science, Engineering, Physics, Math, Operations Research or related) or equivalent experience Strong machine learning and algorithmic background with a deep understanding of supervised and unsupervised learning methods, deep learning, LLMs. 4 years of experience building ML and/or AI driven products or other similar related functions (e.g. software engineering, data science, advanced analytics). Advanced degrees in relevant fields may be counted towards experience requirements. 2 years experience working on industrial-grade Search, RecSys, Personalization or Adtech. Strong skills in SQL and at least 1 programming language (e.g., Python, Julia) Preferred qualifications Master's degree or Ph.D in a highly quantitative field (Computer Science, Engineering, Physics, Math, Operations Research or related) Experience building production grade ML models using one or more Deep Learning frameworks like TensorFlow, Keras, PyTorch, etc. Cross-functional experience in deploying realtime low-latency ML products over GCP or AWS What’s in it for you We’re committed to helping you thrive at work and at home with benefits that support your total well‑being, including: Competitive pay Generous employee discount Physical and mental well‑being support About us As part of the Best Buy team, you’ll help us fulfill our purpose to enrich lives through technology. We bring that to life by humanizing and personalizing tech solutions for every stage of life — in stores, online, and in customers’ homes. Our culture is built on supporting our people and creating space for you to bring your full, authentic self to work. Tomorrow works here.™ Best Buy is an equal opportunity employer. # Lead Data Scientist – Generative AI (GenAI)

Posted Jun 8, 2026

Lead Data Scientist – Generative AI (GenAI)

Experience: 12–15+ Years (Data Science / AI Experience)

Employment Type: Full-Time (W2 Only)

### Location: USA (Hybrid / Onsite)

Work Authorization: H4 EAD, L2S, GC EAD, Green Card, US Citizen

Job Summary

We are seeking a highly accomplished Lead Data Scientist with strong Generative AI expertise to design and drive enterprise-scale AI solutions. The ideal candidate will have deep experience in machine learning, statistical modeling, Large Language Models (LLMs), and advanced analytics, along with strong leadership capabilities to guide teams and deliver impactful AI-driven products.

This role requires ownership of GenAI solution design, model development, experimentation, deployment, and performance optimization across enterprise environments.

- Key Responsibilities
- Define and lead GenAI and data science strategy across business use cases.
- Design and implement LLM-based applications including RAG architectures.
- Lead development of machine learning models and advanced analytics solutions.
- Perform deep exploratory data analysis (EDA) and derive actionable insights.
- Architect end-to-end ML pipelines (data ingestion → training → deployment → monitoring).
- Optimize models for accuracy, scalability, and cost efficiency.
- Collaborate with Data Engineers, ML Engineers, and Product stakeholders.
- Establish best practices for MLOps, model governance, and evaluation frameworks.
- Mentor junior data scientists and lead technical decision-making.
- Drive innovation in AI/ML and Generative AI solutions.

Required Technical Skills

- Data Science & ML
- Strong expertise in Machine Learning & Statistical Modeling
- Python (NumPy, Pandas, Scikit-learn)
- Feature engineering, model evaluation, and tuning

- Generative AI
- LLMs (OpenAI, Anthropic, LLaMA, etc.)
- Prompt engineering and LLM orchestration
- RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)
- LangChain / LlamaIndex / Hugging Face

- Data & Processing
- SQL
- Large-scale data handling and transformation

- Cloud & MLOps
- AWS / Azure / GCP
- MLflow / SageMaker / Azure ML
- CI/CD for ML pipelines
- Model deployment and monitoring

- Preferred Qualifications
- Experience with Deep Learning (TensorFlow / PyTorch)
- Exposure to vector databases (Pinecone, FAISS, Chroma)
- Experience with Databricks or big data platforms
- Strong leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills
